qa_pairs.question: How do you start Ansys CFX in graphical mode on an On Demand Desktop, and what graphics options are available based on GPU availability?
qa_pairs.answer: To start Ansys CFX from the command line of an On Demand Desktop, first load the required module (e.g., `module load StdEnv/2023 ansys/2025R1`). Then, if no GPU was requested when the desktop was started, use the command `cfx5 -graphics mesa`. If a GPU was requested, use `cfx5 -graphics ogl`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to launch the Mechanical APDL graphical user interface (GUI) from the command line in an On Demand Desktop environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To start the Mechanical APDL GUI from the command line in an On Demand Desktop, first load the necessary module, for example, `module load StdEnv/2023 ansys/2022R2` (or a newer version). You can then launch the GUI by typing `mapdl -g` or by typing `launcher` and clicking the `RUN` button. These steps are applicable whether your On Demand Desktop session was started with or without a GPU.
